nanoparticles.name: Lipid Nanoparticle (LNP)
nanoparticles.description: Ionizable lipid nanoparticle for mRNA delivery
nanoparticles.advantages: High transfection efficiency, clinically validated (COVID-19 vaccines)

nanoparticles.name: Gold Nanoparticle (AuNP)
nanoparticles.description: Gold-based nanoparticle for imaging and photothermal therapy

nanoparticles.name: Polymeric Nanoparticle (PLGA)
nanoparticles.description: Biodegradable polymer nanoparticle for controlled release
nanoparticles.limitations: Acidic degradation products, limited for large biomolecules

nanoparticles.name: Silica Nanoparticle (MSN)
nanoparticles.description: Mesoporous silica for high drug loading capacity
nanoparticles.advantages: High surface area, tunable pore size, stable
nanoparticles.limitations: Clearance concerns, potential toxicity at high doses

nanoparticles.name: Liposome
nanoparticles.limitations: Stability issues, rapid clearance without PEGylation

nanoparticles.name: Quantum Dot (QD)
nanoparticles.limitations: Potential heavy metal toxicity, limited clinical use

nanoparticles.name: Carbon Nanotube (CNT)
nanoparticles.description: Cylindrical carbon structure with high surface area
nanoparticles.advantages: High drug loading, unique optical properties

nanoparticles.name: Dendrimer
nanoparticles.limitations: Potential toxicity from positive charges, complex synthesis

nanoparticles.name: Metal-Organic Framework (MOF)
nanoparticles.description: Porous crystalline material with metal ions and organic linkers
nanoparticles.advantages: Ultra-high porosity, tunable structure, high loading
nanoparticles.limitations: Stability in physiological conditions, newer technology

nanoparticles.name: Exosome
nanoparticles.advantages: Natural biocompatibility, immune evasion, cell-specific targeting
